items.title: Head of Product
items.org: Family & Medical Leave Insurance (FAMLI), Maryland Department of Labor
items.description: Third hire to the Digital Service team building out a greenfield digital-first service to support Maryland workers and employers in the rollout of paid family and medical leave for the state.
leadership: Shaped program strategy and led inclusive, consultative processes that built shared understanding across technical, policy, and leadership stakeholders
Designed and owned the planning cadence, maintaining a single source of truth for open decisions and risks and ensuring senior leadership had consistent, executive-ready progress updates
Scaled team of product, engineering, and design professionals from 3 to 17, including developing hiring strategy and processes and onboarding systems
Coached and managed 9 senior members of the Digital Services team, including other managers
Led build vs. buy strategy and oversaw $15.6M in annual vendor contracts, coordinating cross-functional alignment across product, engineering, policy, and legal on foundational technology decisions
delivery: Coordinated cross-functional launch readiness for the FAMLI soft launch, aligning communications, outreach, customer support, and delivery teams ahead of key milestones and ensuring all stakeholders were prepared
Managed program-wide tracking of interdependencies and risks, surfacing blockers early and synthesizing status into communications for senior leadership

items.title: Engineering Manager
items.description: Founded in 2012, Panorama Education helps students achieve academic success and well-being and gives K-12 schools and school districts a platform to hear what families, students, and teachers need in the classroom.
leadership: Led change management for significant organizational shifts, including new team structures and revised processes — communicating proactively and adjusting course based on team feedback
Developed new strategy and processes for how the engineering team prioritized and resolved customer support requests that improved cross-functional communication and overall efficiency
Refined the promotion process for all engineers and led the collaborative biannual review process
Coached and developed mainly senior engineers, including facilitating promotions for 4 engineers over 3 quarters
delivery: Led delivery across 3 cross-functional teams, coordinating product, engineering, and design to achieve predictable delivery and releases and ensure impact for users in 1,500+ school systems nationwide
culture: Rebooted a dormant affinity group for women, gender-nonconforming, and nonbinary employees, providing a point of connection and support for team members

items.title: Engineering Manager, Front-end
items.description: Founded in 2012, Lumen Learning uses open educational resources (OER) to provide affordable course materials that improve learning and replace expensive textbooks in high-enrollment college courses. Over 400 institutions across the U.S. use Lumen's award-winning digital courseware.
leadership: Designed the engineering career framework from scratch — defining leveling criteria, role expectations, and growth pathways to provide the team clarity and the organization a foundation for equitable performance management
Scaled front-end team from 2 to 4 engineers, building hiring and onboarding processes rigorous and successful enough that the broader engineering org adopted them as standard practice
Designed and facilitated team meeting rhythms, including sprint planning and retrospectives, to keep a distributed team aligned and moving
delivery: Partnered with product and design to scope and sequence the roadmap, balancing delivery commitments with team capacity
Partnered with content and curriculum teams to establish standards for accessible, inclusive markup, connecting technical requirements to the quality and compliance needs of the broader product
culture: Conducted company-wide workshop on accessibility and inclusive design principles
Established and modeled engineering team norms, specifically around learning, professional development, and employee wellness

items.title: Adjunct Professor
responsibilities: Instructing undergraduate students in GD 220 (Graphic Design 2) and GD 431 (Front End Web development)

items.title: Front-end Lead + Manager
items.description: Fractured Atlas supports over 500,000 artists and arts organizations handle the business side of their work, like raising money, tracking donors, and selling event tickets.
leadership: Served as project lead for crowdfunding and fundraising tool (over $25 million annually)
Managed UX engineering team, including holding 1:1s and setting team priorities
